---
title: "Benchmark of exploratory data analysis methods for scRNA-seq data"
author: "Christian Halter"
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(),'%e de %B, %Y')`"
output: 
  rmdformats::downcute:
    lightbox: true
    thumbnails: false
    self_contained: true
    gallery: true
    code_folding: show
  pkgdown:
    as_is: true
---

This script prepares datasets for downstream analysis

# Load libraries and set parameters

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
# renv::init()
# renv::snapshot()

source("functions.R")
source("Processed_dataset_metadata.R")


# devtools::install_github("GfellerLab/EPIC", build_vignettes=TRUE)
library(EPIC)
# BiocManager::install("MOFA2")

# reticulate::virtualenv_create(envname = "C:/R_Envs/my_env", version = "3.11.9")
# # Define the path first
# my_path <- "C:/R_Envs/my_env"
# 
# # Install BEFORE the environment is loaded into the R session memory
# reticulate::py_install(
#     packages = "pandas==2.0.3",
#     envname = my_path,
#     method = "virtualenv",
#     pip = TRUE
# )
# reticulate::py_install(
#     packages = "h5py==3.12.1",
#     envname = my_path,
#     method = "virtualenv",
#     pip = TRUE
# )
# reticulate::py_install(
#     packages = "numpy==1.26.4",
#     envname = my_path,
#     method = "virtualenv",
#     pip = TRUE
# )
# reticulate::py_install(
#     packages = "mofapy2",
#     envname = my_path,
#     method = "virtualenv",
#     pip = TRUE
# )
library(reticulate)
use_virtualenv("C:/R_Envs/my_env", required = TRUE)
py_config()
# py_install("mofapy2")
import("mofapy2")
# library(basilisk)
library("MOFA2")
# BiocManager::install("fgsea")
# BiocManager::install("sva")
# devtools::install_github("kharchenkolab/scITD")
library(scITD)
# install.packages("ncdf4")
# BiocManager::install("GloScope")
library(GloScope)
library(funkyheatmap)
library(RColorBrewer)

library(forcats)
library(gtools)

# Set paths
path_nas <- file.path("", "Volumes", "Shared", "DataCollections", "Standardized_SingleCell_Datasets")
# path_data <- file.path(path_root, "data")
path_data <- file.path(getwd(), "data")
path_plots <- file.path(getwd(), "plots")
seurat_file_ending <- "_ECODAprocessed.rds"

set.seed(123)
```

# Create results list to fill

```{r}
result_file <- "result_list.rds"

if (file.exists(result_file)) {
  result_list <- readRDS(result_file)
} else {
  result_list <- list()
}
```


# ---------------------------------------------------------------


# Process datasets

```{r message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo=FALSE, results='hide'}
for (ds in names(datasets)) {
  seurat <- readRDS(file.path(path_data, paste0(datasets[[ds]][["ds_name"]], seurat_file_ending)))
  seurat <- replace_HiTMElayer3_annot(seurat, thresh = 0.1)
  
  seurat@misc$label_col <- datasets[[ds]][["label_col"]]
  seurat@misc$low_res_ct_col <- datasets[[ds]][["low_res_ct_col"]]
  seurat@misc$hi_res_ct_col <- datasets[[ds]][["hi_res_ct_col"]]
  
  result_list <- run_analyses(result_list, ds, seurat, path_data, path_plots)
}
```

# ---------------------------------------------------------------

# Gong and Sharma

```{r}
ds_name <- "GongSharma_2024_PrePrintTBD"

path_root <- file.path("~", "Dropbox", "CSI", "Standardized_SingleCell_Datasets", ds_name)
path_output <- file.path(path_root, "output")
```

### Check for batch effect

```{r}
alpha <- 0.3

meta_cols_plot <- c(
  "Sample",
  "RNA_snn_res.0.5",
  "batch_id",
  "pool_id",
  "chip_id",
  "well_id",
  "cohort.cohortGuid",
  "AIFI_L1",
  "AIFI_L2",
  "AIFI_L3",
  "subject.cmv"
)

# Check in broad cell types (strong global batch effect)

Idents(seurat) <- "Sample"
sub <- subset(x = seurat, downsample = 100)

sub <- sub |>
  NormalizeData() |>
  FindVariableFeatures(nfeatures = 2000) |>
  ScaleData() |>
  RunPCA(dims = 1:50, verbose = FALSE) |>
  FindNeighbors() |>
  FindClusters(resolution = 0.5) |>
  RunUMAP(dims = 1:50)

for (mc in meta_cols_plot) {
  Idents(sub) <- mc
  DimPlot(sub, reduction = "umap", alpha = alpha)
}

# No strong batch effect observed


# To check for more subtle batch effect, we can subset to only T cells, where the gene expression difference between cells is smaller
# Thus, by subseting to only T cells, we should be better able to see smaller batch effects

sub <- subset(x = seurat, subset = AIFI_L1 == "T cell")
sub <- subset(x = sub, downsample = 1000)

sub <- sub |>
  NormalizeData() |>
  FindVariableFeatures(nfeatures = 2000) |>
  ScaleData() |>
  RunPCA(dims = 1:50, verbose = FALSE) |>
  FindNeighbors() |>
  FindClusters(resolution = 0.5) |>
  RunUMAP(dims = 1:50)

for (mc in meta_cols_plot) {
  Idents(sub) <- mc
  DimPlot(sub, reduction = "umap", alpha = alpha)
}

# No weak batch effect observed
```

## Age (females cmvneg)

```{r}
ds <- "GongSharma_age_females_cmvneg"

elem_null <- c()
for (a in analysis_elems) {
  elem_null <- c(elem_null, is.null(result_list[[a]][[ds]]))
  
  if (is.null(result_list[[a]][[ds]])) {
    print(paste("To be analyzed:", a, ds))
  }
}

# If any analyses missing, process dataset for missing analyses
if (any(elem_null)) {

  seurat <- load_h5ad_to_seurat("data/Gongsharma_age_females_cmvneg.h5ad")
  seurat$Sample <- seurat$specimen.specimenGuid
  
  seurat$age <- as.factor(ifelse(seurat$sample.subjectAgeAtDraw < 50, "young", "old"))

  seurat@misc$label_col <- "age"
  seurat@misc$low_res_ct_col <- "AIFI_L1"
  seurat@misc$hi_res_ct_col <- "AIFI_L3"
  
  result_list <- run_analyses(result_list, ds, seurat, path_data, path_plots, factors_test)
}
```

# ---------------------------------------------------------------

# Bassez

# IMPORTANT: Needs automated annotation, as seurat\$cohort c("neoadjuvant_chemo", "treatment_naive") seem to be separately and differently annotated by the authors

## Has also other non-immune cells

```{r}
ds <- "Bassez"

elem_null <- c()
for (a in analysis_elems) {
  elem_null <- c(elem_null, is.null(result_list[[a]][[ds]]))

  if (is.null(result_list[[a]][[ds]])) {
    print(paste("To be analyzed:", a, ds))
  }
}

# If any analyses missing, process dataset for missing analyses
if (any(elem_null)) {

  ds_name <- "BassezA_2021_33958794"

  path_root <- file.path(path_nas, ds_name)
  path_output <- file.path(path_root, "output")

  seurat <- readRDS(file.path(path_output, sprintf("%swhole.rds", ds_name)))

  seurat <- subset(seurat, subset = timepoint == "Pre")
  seurat <- subset(seurat, subset = expansion != "n/a")
  seurat <- subset(seurat, subset = cohort == "treatment_naive")

  seurat$Sample <- seurat$sample
  seurat$cellSubType[is.na(seurat$cellSubType)] <- seurat$cellType[is.na(seurat$cellSubType)]

  convert_seurat_to_h5ad(seurat, ds)

  seurat@misc$label_col <- "expansion"
  seurat@misc$low_res_ct_col <- "cellType"
  seurat@misc$hi_res_ct_col <- "cellSubType"

  result_list <- run_analyses(result_list, ds, seurat, path_data, path_plots, factors_test)
}
```


# ---------------------------------------------------------------
# ---------------------------------------------------------------

# Create list to export raw data to excel

```{r}
df_results_list <- list()
```

# ---------------------------------------------------------------

# ---------------------------------------------------------------

# Plot benchmark results

## Save and flatten the nested list into a DataFrame

```{r}
bmark_result_list <- result_list[["bmark"]]

bmark_result_list$GongSharma <- bmark_result_list$Gongsharma_cmv_young_males
bmark_result_list$Gongsharma_cmv_young_males <- NULL

df_results <- imap_dfr(bmark_result_list, ~ {
  dataset_name <- .y
  imap_dfr(.x, ~ {
    method_name <- .y
    if ("scores" %in% names(.x)) {
      n_samples <- nrow(.x$feat_mat)
      n_features <- ncol(.x$feat_mat)
      
      imap_dfr(.x$scores, ~ {
        tibble(
          dataset = dataset_name,
          method = method_name,
          score = .y,
          value = .x,
          n_samples = n_samples,
          n_features = n_features
        )
      })
    } else {
      tibble() # Return an empty tibble if "scores" is not found
    }
  })
})

# Drop old modularity score method
df_results <- df_results[!df_results$score == "mod_score", ]
```

## Show + sort all tested conditions

```{r}
levs <- mixedsort(as.character(unique(df_results$method)))
levs

df_results$method <- factor(df_results$method, levels = levs)

df_results$dataset[df_results$dataset == "GongSharma_females_cmv"] <- "GongSharma_cmv"
df_results$dataset[df_results$dataset == "GongSharma_females_cmvneg_age"] <- "GongSharma_age"

df_results_list[["Benchmark"]] <- df_results
```

## Plot results

### All methods (overview)

```{r fig.height=8, fig.width=16}
# New facet label names for dose variable
score.labs <- c("ANOSIM score", "Adjusted rand index", "Modularity score")
names(score.labs) <- sort(unique(df_results$score))

# New facet label names for supp variable
datas.labs <- sort(unique(df_results$dataset))
datas.labs[datas.labs == "GongSharma_cmv_females"] <- "Gong & Sharma\nSubset: females\nSeparation by: CMV infection"
datas.labs[datas.labs == "GongSharma_age_females_cmvneg"] <- "Gong & Sharma\nSubset: CMV negative females\nSeparation by: age"
names(datas.labs) <- sort(unique(df_results$dataset))


ggplot(df_results, aes(x = method, y = value)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge()) +
  labs(x = "",
       y = "Separation") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1),
        legend.position = "none") +
  facet_grid(score ~ dataset,
             scales = "free",
             labeller = labeller(score = score.labs, dataset = datas.labs))
  # facet_grid(scales = "free",
  #            labeller = labeller(score = score.labs, dataset = datas.labs),
  #            rows = vars(score), cols = vars(dataset))
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "benchmark_detailed_barplot.pdf"),
       width = 150,
       height = 7, limitsize = FALSE)


df_results %>%
  group_by(score, method) %>%
  summarize(mean = mean(value), sem = sd(value) / sqrt(length(value))) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  
  ggplot(aes(x = method, y = mean)) + 
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge()) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=mean-sem, ymax=mean+sem), width=0.2, colour="black", alpha=0.9) +
  labs(x = "", y = "") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 75, vjust = 1, hjust=1)) +
  facet_grid(scales = "free",
             rows = vars(score),
             labeller = labeller(score = score.labs))
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "benchmark_summary_barplot_separatescores_bars.pdf"),
       width = 14,
       height = 7)
```

### Supp fig 16: Benchmark unscaled metrics

```{r fig.width=12, fig.height=5}
filter_methods <- c(
  "ECODA_authors_HR",
  "ECODA_authors_HR_NULL",
  # "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer2",
  # "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer3",
  # "ECODA_seuratres_2",
  # "ECODA_PB_combo_normquantile_ecodaweight0.5",
  # paste0("ECODA_PB_combo_norm", norm_methods, "_ecodaweight0.5"),
  # "ECODA_authors_LR",
  # "Avg_PCA_embedding",
  # "Freq_highres",
  # "ECODA_deconv",
  # "Pseudobulk_10_PCA_dims",
  "Pseudobulk_hvg2000",
  # "Pseudobulk_hvg2000_bl",
  # "Pseudobulk_schvg2000",
  "MOFA_hvg2000_factors15",
  "scITD_hvg2000_factors5",
  "MrVI_hvg2000",
  "scPoli_hvg2000_dims5",
  "PILOT_hvg2000",
  "GloScope_hvg2000_pcadims30_sqrtmat",
  "GloProp"
)


df_plot <- df_results %>%
  filter(method %in% filter_methods) %>%
  mutate(method = recode(method,
                         "ECODA_authors_HR" = "ECODA_authors",
                         "ECODA_authors_HR_NULL" = "Shuffled_baseline",
                         "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer2" = "ECODA_automated",
                         # "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer3" = "ECODA_automated_(HiTME_layer3)",
                         "ECODA_seuratres_2" = "ECODA_unsupervised",
                         # "ECODA_PB_combo_normquantile_ecodaweight0.5" = "ECODA+Pseudobulk",
                         "Pseudobulk_hvg2000" = "Pseudobulk",
                         "Freq_highres" = "ECODA_Freq%",
                         "MOFA_hvg2000_factors15" = "MOFA",
                         "scITD_hvg2000_factors5" = "scITD",
                         "MrVI_hvg2000" = "MrVI",
                         "scPoli_hvg2000_dims5" = "scPoli",
                         "PILOT_hvg2000" = "PILOT",
                         "GloScope_hvg2000_pcadims30_sqrtmat" = "GloScope"))

pointsize <- 1.5

# Plot 1: ANOSIM
p1 <- df_plot %>%
  filter(score == "anosim_score") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = fct_reorder(method, value, .desc = TRUE, .fun = mean), y = value)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_se", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", fill = "white", color = "black") +
  geom_point(aes(shape = dataset, color = dataset), size = pointsize) + # Color added for legend consistency
  scale_shape_manual(values=1:length(unique((df_plot$dataset)))) +
  geom_line(aes(group=dataset, linetype=dataset, color = dataset), alpha = 1) +
  labs(x = "", y = "ANOSIM score") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 65, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))

# Plot 2: Modularity
p2 <- df_plot %>%
  filter(grepl(pattern = "mod", score)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = fct_reorder(method, value, .desc = TRUE, .fun = mean), y = value)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_se", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", fill = "white", color = "black") +
  geom_point(aes(shape = dataset, color = dataset), size = pointsize) + # Color added for legend consistency
  scale_shape_manual(values=1:length(unique((df_plot$dataset)))) +
  geom_line(aes(group=dataset, linetype=dataset, color = dataset), alpha = 1) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Modularity score") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 65, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))

# Plot 3: ARI
p3 <- df_plot %>%
  filter(score == "cluster_score") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = fct_reorder(method, value, .desc = TRUE, .fun = mean), y = value)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_se", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", fill = "white", color = "black") +
  geom_point(aes(shape = dataset, color = dataset), size = pointsize) + # Color added for legend consistency
  scale_shape_manual(values=1:length(unique((df_plot$dataset)))) +
  geom_line(aes(group=dataset, linetype=dataset, color = dataset), alpha = 1) +
  labs(x = "", y = "ARI score") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 65, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))

# --- Combine using Patchwork ---

combined_plot <- (p1 | p2 | p3) + 
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") & # Collects all legends into one
  theme(legend.position = "right") # Places the shared legend at the bottom
combined_plot

# Save the combined plot
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "Supp_fig_16_benchmark_unscaled_metrics_perdataset.pdf"), 
       plot = combined_plot,
       width = 12, # Increased width to fit 3 plots
       height = 5)
```

```{r fig.height=4, fig.width=4}
filter_methods <- c(
  "ECODA_authors_HR",
  "ECODA_authors_HR_NULL",
  "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer2",
  "ECODA_seuratres_2",
  "Avg_PCA_embedding",
  "Freq_highres",
  "Pseudobulk_10_PCA_dims",
  "MOFA_hvg2000_factors15",
  "scITD_hvg2000_factors5",
  "MrVI_hvg2000",
  "scPoli_hvg2000_dims5",
  "PILOT_hvg2000",
  "GloScope_hvg2000_pcadims30"
)


df_plot_scaled <- df_results %>%
  filter(method %in% filter_methods) %>%
  # -- Recoding names --
  mutate(method = recode(method,
                         "ECODA_authors_HR" = "ECODA_authors",
                         "ECODA_authors_HR_NULL" = "Shuffled_baseline",
                         "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer2" = "ECODA_automated",
                         "ECODA_seuratres_2" = "ECODA_Leiden_clustering",
                         "Pseudobulk_10_PCA_dims" = "Pseudobulk",
                         "Freq_highres" = "ECODA_Freq%",
                         "MOFA_hvg2000_factors15" = "MOFA",
                         "scITD_hvg2000_factors5" = "scITD",
                         "MrVI_hvg2000" = "MrVI",
                         "scPoli_hvg2000_dims5" = "scPoli",
                         "PILOT_hvg2000" = "PILOT",
                         "GloScope_hvg2000_pcadims30" = "GloScope")) %>%
  # -- CRITICAL STEP: Min-Max Scaling per Dataset and Score --
  group_by(dataset, score) %>% 
  mutate(scaled_value = min_max(value)) %>% 
  ungroup()

# 4. Generate the Plot
# We filter for ANOSIM and use 'scaled_value' instead of 'value'
df_plot_scaled %>%
  filter(score == "anosim_score") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = fct_reorder(method, scaled_value, .desc = TRUE, .fun = mean), 
             y = scaled_value)) + # Use the scaled column
  
  # Error bars and Mean Bar
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_se", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", fill = "white", color = "black") +
  
  # Individual Points and connecting lines (Paired data)
  geom_point(position = "identity", alpha = 0.6, size = 0.8) +
  geom_line(aes(group = dataset), color = "grey20", alpha = 0.2) +
  
  # Statistical Test
  stat_compare_means(
    ref.group = "ECODA_authors",
    method = "wilcox.test",
    paired = TRUE,
    angle = 25,
    vjust = 1,
    aes(label = sprintf("%5.2f", as.numeric(..p.format..)))
  ) +
  
  # Adjust Y-axis (Since data is 0-1, we set limit slightly higher for p-values)
  ylim(c(0, 1.15)) + 
  
  labs(x = "", y = "Separation score\n(Scaled ANOSIM score)") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 65, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "benchmark_summary_barplot_ANOSIM_minmaxscaled_mainmethods_refECODA.pdf"),
       width = 4.5,
       height = 4.5)
```


#### Plot all separation metrics together

```{r fig.height=6, fig.width=5}
df_results %>%
  filter(method %in% filter_methods) %>%
  mutate(method = recode(method,
                         "ECODA_authors_HR" = "ECODA_authors",
                         "ECODA_authors_HR_NULL" = "Shuffled_baseline",
                         "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer2" = "ECODA_automated",
                         "ECODA_seuratres_2" = "ECODA_unsupervised",
                         "Pseudobulk_10_PCA_dims" = "Pseudobulk",
                         "Freq_highres" = "Cell_type_composition_%",
                         "MOFA_hvg2000_factors15" = "MOFA",
                         "scITD_hvg2000_factors5" = "scITD",
                         "MrVI_hvg2000" = "MrVI",
                         "scPoli_hvg2000" = "scPoli",
                         "scITD_2_factors" = "scITD",
                         "PILOT_hvg2000" = "PILOT",
                         "GloScope_hvg2000_pcadims30_sqrtmat" = "GloScope")) %>%
  
  group_by(method, score) %>%
  mutate(mean_value = mean(value[score == "anosim_score"])) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  
  ggplot(aes(x = fct_reorder(method, mean_value, .desc = TRUE),
             y = value, 
             fill = ifelse(grepl(default_methods_regex, method), "cornflowerblue", "gray70"))) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_se", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar") +
  labs(x = "", y = "") +
  theme_classic2(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_markdown(angle = 65, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
  panel.grid.major.y = element_line(size = 0.25, linetype = 'solid',
                                colour = "lightgrey"), 
  panel.grid.minor.y = element_line(size = 0.25, linetype = 'solid',
                                colour = "lightgrey")) +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  # ylim(c(-0.4, 1)) +
  facet_grid(scales = "free_x",
             space='free',
             rows = vars(score),
             labeller = labeller(score = score.labs))
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "benchmark_summary_barplot_allmetricscombined_mainmethods_with_bad_baselines.pdf"),
       width = 4.5,
       height = 6.5)
```

#### Supp fig 18: Plot PCA for selected datasets and main methods

```{r}
ds <- "Kfoury"

m <- "ECODA_authors_HR"
p1 <- plot_pca(
  result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["feat_mat"]],
  labels = result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["labels"]],
  coord_equal = TRUE, repel = TRUE, cluster_score = FALSE, mod_score = FALSE,
  title = m, n_ct_show = 5, invisible = c("quali")
)
print(p1)

plot_mds(
  result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["dist_mat"]],
  labels = result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["labels"]],
  coord_equal = TRUE, cluster_score = FALSE, mod_score = FALSE,
  title = m
)

m <- "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer2"
p2 <- plot_pca(
    result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["feat_mat"]],
    labels = result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["labels"]],
    coord_equal = TRUE, repel = TRUE, cluster_score = FALSE, mod_score = FALSE,
    title = "ECODA_HiTME", n_ct_show = 5, invisible = c("quali")
  )
print(p2)

m <- "ECODA_scATOMIC_HR"
p3 <- plot_pca(
    result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["feat_mat"]],
    labels = result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["labels"]],
    coord_equal = TRUE, repel = TRUE, cluster_score = FALSE, mod_score = FALSE,
    title = "ECODA_scATOMIC", n_ct_show = 5, invisible = c("quali")
  )
print(p3)

p_kfoury <- wrap_plots(list(p1, p2, p3)) +
  plot_layout(nrow = 1, guides = "collect")
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, paste0("Supp_fig_18_Kfoury_PCA_authors_vs_auto_", ds, ".pdf")),
       width = 15,
       height = 6)


ds <- "Wu"

m <- "ECODA_authors_HR"
p1 <- plot_pca(
    result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["feat_mat"]],
    labels = result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["labels"]],
    coord_equal = TRUE, repel = TRUE, cluster_score = FALSE, mod_score = FALSE,
    title = m, n_ct_show = 5, invisible = c("quali")
  )
print(p1)

m <- "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer2"
p2 <- plot_pca(
    result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["feat_mat"]],
    labels = result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["labels"]],
    coord_equal = TRUE, repel = TRUE, cluster_score = FALSE, mod_score = FALSE,
    title = "ECODA_HiTME", n_ct_show = 5, invisible = c("quali")
  )
print(p2)

m <- "ECODA_scATOMIC_HR"
p3 <- plot_pca(
    result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["feat_mat"]],
    labels = result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["labels"]],
    coord_equal = TRUE, repel = TRUE, cluster_score = FALSE, mod_score = FALSE,
    title = "ECODA_scATOMIC", n_ct_show = 5, invisible = c("quali")
  )
print(p3)

p_wu <- wrap_plots(list(p1, p2, p3)) +
  plot_layout(nrow = 1, guides = "collect")
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, paste0("Supp_fig_18_Wu_PCA_authors_vs_auto_", ds, ".pdf")),
       width = 15,
       height = 6)

p_combined <- wrap_plots(list(p_kfoury + labs(tag = "Kfoury"), p_wu + labs(tag = "Wu"))) +
  plot_layout(nrow = 2) & 
  theme(plot.tag = element_text(face = "bold"))
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, paste0("Supp_fig_18_combined.pdf")),
       width = 15,
       height = 6)
```

```{r fig.height=12, fig.width=12}
ds <- "Pelka"
plot_list <- list()

methods <- list(
  "ECODA_authors_HR" = "ECODA",
  "ECODA_authors_HR_NULL" = "Shuffled_baseline",
  "Pseudobulk_10_PCA_dims" = "Pseudobulk",
  "MOFA_hvg2000_factors15" = "MOFA",
  "scITD_hvg2000_factors5" = "scITD",
  "MrVI_hvg2000" = "MrVI",
  "scPoli_hvg2000_dims5" = "scPoli",
  "PILOT_hvg2000" = "PILOT",
  "GloScope_hvg2000_pcadims30_sqrtmat" = "GloScope"
)

for (m in names(methods)) {
  dist_mat <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["dist_mat"]]
  labels <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["labels"]]
  
  if (m == "ECODA_authors_HR_NULL") {
    labels <- sample(labels)
  }
  
  plot_list[[m]] <- plot_pca(
    feat_mat = dist_mat,
    labels = labels,
    coord_equal = FALSE,
    title = methods[[m]]
  )
}

wrap_plots(plot_list) +
  plot_layout(nrow = 3, guides = "collect")
```


### Plot PCA for all datasets (PB and ECODA)

```{r fig.height=4, fig.width=8}
methods <- list(
  "Pseudobulk_hvg2000" = "Pseudobulk",
  # "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer2" = "ECODA_automated",
  "ECODA_authors_HR" = "ECODA"
)

for (ds in names(result_list[["bmark"]])) {
  plot_list <- list()
  
  for (m in names(methods)) {
    feat_mat <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["feat_mat"]]
    labels <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["labels"]]
    
    if (ds=="Bassez") levels(labels) <- c("Responders", "Non-responders")
    if (ds=="Zhang") levels(labels) <- tools::toTitleCase(levels(labels))
    
    if (methods[[m]] == "ECODA") title <- "Cell type composition (ECODA)" else title <- methods[[m]]
    
    plot_list[[m]] <- plot_pca(
      feat_mat = feat_mat,
      labels = labels,
      cluster_score = FALSE, mod_score = FALSE, anosim_score = FALSE,
      coord_equal = FALSE,
      n_ct_show = 5, invisible = "quali", repel = TRUE,
      title = title
    )
    
    if (ds=="Bassez") {
      plot_list[[m]] <- plot_list[[m]] +
        scale_color_discrete(
          name = "Treatment response", 
          labels = labels
        ) +
        scale_shape_discrete(
          name = "Treatment response",
          labels = labels
        )
    }
    if (ds=="Zhang") {
      plot_list[[m]] <- plot_list[[m]] +
        scale_color_discrete(
          name = "Tissue", 
          labels = labels
        ) +
        scale_shape_discrete(
          name = "Tissue", 
          labels = labels
        )
    }
    
  }
  
  wrap_plots(plot_list) +
    plot_layout(nrow = 1, guides = "collect")
  
  ggsave(file.path(path_plots, paste0("benchmark_pca_", ds, ".pdf")),
         width = 8,
         height = 3.5)
}

```


### Supp fig 2-12: MDS for all datasets

```{r fig.height=9, fig.width=11}
methods <- list(
  "ECODA_authors_HR" = "ECODA",
  # "ECODA_seuratres_2" = "ECODA_Leiden_res_2",
  # "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer2" = "ECODA_automated",
  
  # "Freq_highres" = "ECODA_Freq%",
  "Pseudobulk_hvg2000" = "Pseudobulk",
  # "Avg_PCA_embedding" = "Avg_PCA_embedding",
  
  "GloScope_hvg2000_pcadims30_sqrtmat" = "GloScope",
  "GloProp" = "GloProp",
  "MrVI_hvg2000" = "MrVI",
  "scPoli_hvg2000_dims15_highres" = "scPoli",
  
  "MOFA_hvg2000_factors15" = "MOFA",
  "PILOT_hvg2000" = "PILOT",
  "scITD_hvg2000_factors5" = "scITD"
)

idx <- 2

for (ds in names(result_list[["bmark"]])) {
  plot_list <- list()
  
  for (m in names(methods)) {
    dist_mat <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["dist_mat"]]
    labels <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["labels"]]
    
    if (ds == "Bassez") {
      labels <- as.character(labels)
      labels[labels == "E"] <- "Responders"
      labels[labels == "NE"] <- "Non-responders"
      labels <- factor(labels, levels = c("Responders", "Non-responders"))
    }
    
    plot_list[[m]] <- plot_mds(
      dist_mat,
      labels,
      cluster_score = FALSE,mod_score = FALSE, anosim_score = FALSE,
      coord_equal = TRUE,
      title = methods[[m]]
    )
  }
  
  wrap_plots(plot_list) +
    plot_layout(nrow = 3, guides = "collect")
  
  ggsave(file.path(path_plots, paste0("Supp_fig_", idx, "_benchmark_mds_", ds, ".pdf")),
         width = 11,
         height = 9)
  
  idx <- idx + 1
}

```

```{r fig.height=6, fig.width=6}
m <- "PILOT_hvg2000"
ds <- "Zhang"
dist_mat <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["dist_mat"]]
labels <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["labels"]]

plot_mds(
  dist_mat,
  labels,
  cluster_score = FALSE,mod_score = FALSE, anosim_score = FALSE,
  coord_equal = TRUE,
  axes = c(10,11),
  title = methods[[m]]
)
```


### Compare PCA based on features vs dist

```{r fig.height=6, fig.width=18}
ds <- "Pelka"
plot_list <- list()

methods <- list(
  "ECODA_authors_HR" = "ECODA_authors",
  "ECODA_authors_HR_NULL" = "Shuffled_baseline",
  "Freq_highres" = "ECODA_Freq%",
  "ECODA_seuratres_2" = "ECODA_unsupervised",
  "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer2" = "ECODA_automated",
  "Pseudobulk_hvg2000" = "Pseudobulk",
  "MOFA_hvg2000_factors15" = "MOFA",
  "scITD_hvg2000_factors5" = "scITD",
  "MrVI_hvg2000" = "MrVI",
  "scPoli_hvg2000_dims5" = "scPoli",
  "PILOT_hvg2000" = "PILOT",
  "GloScope_hvg2000_pcadims30" = "GloScope"
)

for (m in names(methods)) {
  feat_mat <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["feat_mat"]]
  labels <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["labels"]]
  if (ds == "Pelka") {
    labels <- as.character(labels)
    labels[labels == "N"] <- "Normal"
    labels[labels == "T"] <- "Tumor"
  }
  if (ds == "Bassez") {
    labels <- as.character(labels)
    labels[labels == "E"] <- "Expanders"
    labels[labels == "NE"] <- "Non-expanders"
  }
  
  if (m == "ECODA_authors_HR_NULL") {
    labels <- sample(labels)
  }
  
  avg_sep_score <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]]$scores[c("mod_knn3_score", "anosim_score", "cluster_score")] %>%
    unlist() %>%
    mean() %>%
    round(2)
  
  plot_list[[m]] <- plot_pca(
    feat_mat = feat_mat,
    # cluster_score = F, mod_score = F, anosim_score = F,
    # knn_k = NULL,
    labels = labels,
    coord_equal = FALSE,
    title = paste0(methods[[m]], "\nSeparation score: ", sprintf("%.2f", avg_sep_score))
  )
}

wrap_plots(plot_list) +
  plot_layout(nrow = 2, guides = "collect") +
  plot_annotation(title = ds)
# ggsave(file.path(path_plots, paste0("PCA_", ds, ".pdf")),
#        width = 18,
#        height = 6)

```

```{r}
for (m in names(methods)) {
  dist_mat <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["dist_mat"]]
  labels <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["labels"]]
  if (ds == "Pelka") {
    labels <- as.character(labels)
    labels[labels == "N"] <- "Normal"
    labels[labels == "T"] <- "Tumor"
  }
  
  if (m == "ECODA_authors_HR_NULL") {
    labels <- sample(labels)
  }
  
  plot_list[[m]] <- plot_pca(
    feat_mat = dist_mat,
    cluster_score = F, mod_score = F, anosim_score = F,
    labels = labels,
    coord_equal = FALSE,
    title = methods[[m]]
  )
}

wrap_plots(plot_list) +
  plot_layout(nrow = 2, guides = "collect") +
  plot_annotation(title = ds)


for (m in names(methods)) {
  dist_mat <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["dist_mat"]]
  labels <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["labels"]]
  if (ds == "Pelka") {
    labels <- as.character(labels)
    labels[labels == "N"] <- "Normal"
    labels[labels == "T"] <- "Tumor"
  }
  
  if (m == "ECODA_authors_HR_NULL") {
    labels <- sample(labels)
  }
  
  plot_list[[m]] <- plot_pca(
    feat_mat = dist_mat,
    labels = labels,
    coord_equal = FALSE,
    title = methods[[m]],
    cluster_score = FALSE,
    mod_score = FALSE,
    anosim_score = FALSE
  )
}

wrap_plots(plot_list) +
  plot_layout(nrow = 2, guides = "collect") +
  plot_annotation(title = ds)
```

```{r fig.height=12, fig.width=12}
for (m in names(methods)) {
  feat_mat <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["feat_mat"]]
  labels <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["labels"]]
  
  if (m == "ECODA_authors_HR_NULL") {
    labels <- sample(labels)
  }
  
  plot_list[[m]] <- plot_pca(
    feat_mat = feat_mat,
    labels = labels,
    n_ct_show = 5,
    invisible = "quali",
    coord_equal = FALSE,
    title = methods[[m]]
  )
}

wrap_plots(plot_list) +
  plot_layout(nrow = 3, guides = "collect")

```

### Show example heatmap

```{r fig.height=7, fig.width=7}
library(pheatmap)

ds <- "Bassez"

feat_mat <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR$feat_mat
labels <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR$labels

df_heatmap <- feat_mat %>% scale(center = TRUE, scale = FALSE) %>% t() %>% as.data.frame()
my_sample_col <- list(
  subtype = as.factor(labels)
) %>% as.data.frame()
row.names(my_sample_col) <- colnames(df_heatmap)

pdf(file = file.path(path_plots, paste0("Heatmap_", ds, ".pdf")),
    width = 6,
    height = 6)
pheatmap(
  df_heatmap,
  annotation_col = my_sample_col,
  # cluster_rows = FALSE,
  # cluster_cols = FALSE,
  scale = "none",
  clustering_method = "ward.D2",
  show_colnames = FALSE,
  cutree_rows = 4,
  cutree_cols = 4
)
dev.off()
pheatmap(
  df_heatmap,
  annotation_col = my_sample_col,
  # cluster_rows = FALSE,
  # cluster_cols = FALSE,
  scale = "none",
  clustering_method = "ward.D2",
  show_colnames = FALSE,
  cutree_rows = 4,
  cutree_cols = 4
)

plot_pca(
    feat_mat = feat_mat,
    labels = labels,
    n_ct_show = 5,
    invisible = "quali",
    coord_equal = FALSE,
    title = "",
    repel = TRUE
)
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, paste0("PCA_arrows_", ds, ".pdf")),
       width = 6,
       height = 6)
```

### Figure 3 A: ECODA annotation method comparison
### and Supp fig 17

```{r fig.height=6, fig.width=5}
pointsize <- 1.5

filter_methods <- c(
  "ECODA_authors_HR",
  "ECODA_authors_LR",
  "ECODA_deconv",
  "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer2",
  # "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer3",
  "ECODA_scATOMIC_HR",
  "ECODA_seuratres_0.1",
  "ECODA_seuratres_0.4",
  "ECODA_seuratres_2",
  "ECODA_seuratres_5",
  "ECODA_seuratres_20"
)


df_plot <- df_results %>%
  filter(score == "anosim_score") %>%
  filter(!grepl("Zhang|Lee", dataset)) %>% # Exclude Zhang and Lee, as they do not have high-resolution author annotation
  filter(method %in% filter_methods) %>%
  mutate(method = recode(method,
                         "ECODA_seuratres_0.1" = "ECODA_Leiden_res_0.1",
                         "ECODA_seuratres_0.4" = "ECODA_Leiden_res_0.4",
                         "ECODA_seuratres_2" = "ECODA_Leiden_res_2",
                         "ECODA_seuratres_5" = "ECODA_Leiden_res_5",
                         "ECODA_seuratres_20" = "ECODA_Leiden_res_20",
                         "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer2" = "ECODA_HiTME",
                         "ECODA_scATOMIC_HR" = "ECODA_scATOMIC"
  ))
  
p <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = fct_reorder(method, value, .desc = TRUE, .fun = mean), y = value)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_se", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", fill = "white", color = "black") +
  geom_point(aes(shape = dataset, color = dataset), size = pointsize) + # Color added for legend consistency
  scale_shape_manual(values=1:length(unique((df_plot$dataset)))) +
  geom_line(aes(group=dataset, linetype=dataset, color = dataset), alpha = 1) +
  stat_compare_means(
    ref.group = "ECODA_authors_HR",
    method = "wilcox.test",
    paired = TRUE, # Set to FALSE as the groups are not paired
    aes(label = after_stat(p.signif)),
    size = 3,
    label.y = 1
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Separation score (ANOSIM)") +
  ylim(c(min(df_plot$value), max(df_plot$value) + 0.15)) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 65, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))
print(p)
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "Figure_3_A_annotationmethods_barplot_anosim.pdf"),
       width = 5,
       height = 5)



df_plot <- df_results %>%
  filter(score == "mod_knn3_score") %>%
  filter(!grepl("Zhang|Lee", dataset)) %>% # Exclude Zhang and Lee, as they do not have high-resolution author annotation
  filter(method %in% filter_methods) %>%
  mutate(method = recode(method,
                         "ECODA_seuratres_0.1" = "ECODA_Leiden_res_0.1",
                         "ECODA_seuratres_0.4" = "ECODA_Leiden_res_0.4",
                         "ECODA_seuratres_2" = "ECODA_Leiden_res_2",
                         "ECODA_seuratres_5" = "ECODA_Leiden_res_5",
                         "ECODA_seuratres_20" = "ECODA_Leiden_res_20",
                         "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer2" = "ECODA_HiTME",
                         "ECODA_scATOMIC_HR" = "ECODA_scATOMIC"
  ))
  
p_mod <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = fct_reorder(method, value, .desc = TRUE, .fun = mean), y = value)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_se", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", fill = "white", color = "black") +
  geom_point(aes(shape = dataset, color = dataset), size = pointsize) + # Color added for legend consistency
  scale_shape_manual(values=1:length(unique((df_plot$dataset)))) +
  geom_line(aes(group=dataset, linetype=dataset, color = dataset), alpha = 1) +
  stat_compare_means(
    ref.group = "ECODA_authors_HR",
    method = "wilcox.test",
    paired = TRUE, # Set to FALSE as the groups are not paired
    aes(label = after_stat(p.signif)),
    size = 3,
    label.y = 1.1
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Separation score (Modularity)") +
  ylim(c(min(df_plot$value), max(df_plot$value) + 0.15)) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 65, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))
print(p_mod)
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "benchmark_summary_barplot_modularity_annotationmethods_refECODAhr.pdf"),
       width = 2.5,
       height = 4)


df_plot <- df_results %>%
  filter(score == "cluster_score") %>%
  filter(!grepl("Zhang|Lee", dataset)) %>% # Exclude Zhang and Lee, as they do not have high-resolution author annotation
  filter(method %in% filter_methods) %>%
  mutate(method = recode(method,
                         "ECODA_seuratres_0.1" = "ECODA_Leiden_res_0.1",
                         "ECODA_seuratres_0.4" = "ECODA_Leiden_res_0.4",
                         "ECODA_seuratres_2" = "ECODA_Leiden_res_2",
                         "ECODA_seuratres_5" = "ECODA_Leiden_res_5",
                         "ECODA_seuratres_20" = "ECODA_Leiden_res_20",
                         "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer2" = "ECODA_HiTME",
                         "ECODA_scATOMIC_HR" = "ECODA_scATOMIC"
  ))
  
p_ari <- ggplot(df_plot, aes(x = fct_reorder(method, value, .desc = TRUE, .fun = mean), y = value)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_se", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", fill = "white", color = "black") +
  geom_point(aes(shape = dataset, color = dataset), size = pointsize) + # Color added for legend consistency
  scale_shape_manual(values=1:length(unique((df_plot$dataset)))) +
  geom_line(aes(group=dataset, linetype=dataset, color = dataset), alpha = 1) +
  stat_compare_means(
    ref.group = "ECODA_authors_HR",
    method = "wilcox.test",
    paired = TRUE, # Set to FALSE as the groups are not paired
    aes(label = after_stat(p.signif)),
    size = 3,
    label.y = 1.1
  ) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Separation score (ARI)") +
  ylim(c(min(df_plot$value), max(df_plot$value) + 0.15)) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 65, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))
print(p_ari)
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "benchmark_summary_barplot_ari_annotationmethods_refECODAhr.pdf"),
       width = 2.5,
       height = 4)


wrap_plots(list(p_mod, p_ari)) +
  plot_layout(nrow = 1, guides = "collect")
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "Supp_fig_17_annotationmethods_barplot_mod_ari.pdf"),
       width = 9,
       height = 5)
```

#### Figure 3 B: Plot PCA for selected annot. methods (Adams)

```{r fig.height=8, fig.width=8}
ds <- "Adams"

methods <- list(
  "ECODA_authors_HR" = "ECODA_authors_HR",
  "ECODA_seuratres_5" = "ECODA_Leiden_res_5",
  "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer2" = "ECODA_HiTME",
  "ECODA_authors_LR" = "ECODA_authors_LR"
  # "ECODA_scATOMIC_HR" = "ECODA_scATOMIC",
  # "ECODA_deconv" = "ECODA_deconv"
  # "ECODA_seuratres_0.1" = "ECODA_Leiden_res_0.1"
)

plot_list <- list()

for (m in names(methods)) {
  feat_mat <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["feat_mat"]]
  labels <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["labels"]]
  
  plot_list[[m]] <- plot_pca(
    feat_mat = feat_mat,
    labels = labels,
    cluster_score = FALSE,mod_score = FALSE, anosim_score = FALSE,
    coord_equal = TRUE, pointsize = 2,
    n_ct_show = 5, invisible = "quali", repel = TRUE,
    title = methods[[m]]
  )
}

wrap_plots(plot_list) +
  plot_layout(nrow = 2, guides = "collect")

ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "Figure_3_B_annotationmethods_pca_adams.pdf"),
       width = 8,
       height = 7)
```



#### Supp fig 18: Plot PCA for selected annot. methods (GongSharma)

```{r fig.height=10, fig.width=12}
datas <- c("Bassez", "Lee", "Stephenson")

methods <- list(
  "ECODA_authors_HR" = "ECODA_authors_HR",
  # "ECODA_authors_LR" = "ECODA_authors_LR",
  "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer2" = "ECODA_HiTME",
  "ECODA_scATOMIC_HR" = "ECODA_scATOMIC",
  # "ECODA_deconv" = "ECODA_deconv",
  "ECODA_seuratres_2" = "ECODA_Leiden_res_2"
  # "ECODA_seuratres_0.1" = "ECODA_Leiden_res_0.1"
)

plot_list <- list()

for (ds in datas) {
  pl <- list()
  for (m in names(methods)) {
    if (ds == "Lee" & m == "ECODA_authors_HR") next
    
    dist_mat <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]]$dist_mat
    labels <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]][[m]][["labels"]]
    
    if (ds == "Bassez") {
      labels <- as.character(labels)
      labels[labels == "E"] <- "Responders"
      labels[labels == "NE"] <- "Non-responders"
      labels <- factor(labels, levels = c("Responders", "Non-responders"))
    }
    
    pl[[m]] <- plot_mds(
      dist_mat = dist_mat,
      labels = labels,
      cluster_score = FALSE, mod_score = FALSE, anosim_score = TRUE,
      coord_equal = TRUE,
      title = methods[[m]]
    )
  }
  
  # Wrap the row patchwork in wrap_elements to preserve the annotation
  row_plot <- wrap_plots(pl, nrow = 1, guides = "collect") + 
    plot_annotation(
      title = ds, 
      theme = theme(plot.title = element_text(size = 18, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5))
    )
  
  plot_list[[ds]] <- wrap_elements(row_plot)
}

# Combine the rows vertically
wrap_plots(plot_list, ncol = 1)


ggsave(file.path(path_plots, paste0("Supp_fig_18_Bassez_Lee_Stephenson.pdf")),
       width = 12,
       height = 10)
```


### Supp fig 22: Method parameter screening

```{r fig.height=8, fig.width=14}
filter_methods <- c(
  "ECODA_authors_HR",
  "ECODA_authors_LR",
  "ECODA_deconv",
  "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer2",
  "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer3",
  "ECODA_scATOMIC_HR",
  "ECODA_seuratres_0.4",
  "ECODA_seuratres_2",
  "ECODA_seuratres_5",
  "ECODA_seuratres_20"
)

default_methods <- c(
    "ECODA_authors_HR\\b", 
    "MOFA_hvg2000_factors15", 
    "Pseudobulk_hvg2000\\b",
    "PILOT_hvg2000_HR\\b",
    "scITD_hvg2000_factors5",
    "scPoli_hvg2000_dims15_HR\\b",
    "GloScope_hvg2000_pcadims30",
    "GloProp",
    "MrVI_hvg2000"
)
default_methods_regex <- paste(default_methods, collapse = "|")

score.labs <- c("ANOSIM score", "Adjusted rand index", "Modularity score")
names(score.labs) <- sort(unique(df_results$score))

df_results %>%
  # filter(score == "anosim_score") %>%
  filter(!grepl("HiTME|scATOMIC|seuratres|ECODA_authors_HR_NULL|ECODA_authors_LR|ECODA_deconv|Freq_highres|varexp|varcts|Avg_PCA|combo", method),
         !grepl("GloScope", method) | grepl("sqrtmat", method)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    if_else(grepl("scPoli", method), grepl("highres|lowres", method), TRUE)
  ) %>%
  # filter(method %in% filter_methods) %>%
  # mutate(method = recode(method,
  #                        "MOFA_2_factors" = "MOFA",
  #                        "scITD_2_factors" = "scITD",
  #                        "ECODA_highres" = "ECODA")) %>%
  
  # CREATE THE FACETING GROUP COLUMN
  mutate(
    method_plot = case_when(
      grepl("^ECODA_", method) ~ "ECODA",
      grepl("^MOFA_", method) ~ "MOFA",
      grepl("^MrVI_", method) ~ "MrVI",
      grepl("^scITD_", method) ~ "scITD",
      grepl("^GloScope_", method) ~ "GloScope",
      grepl("^GloProp", method) ~ "GloScope",
      grepl("^PILOT_", method) ~ "PILOT",
      grepl("^scPoli_", method) ~ "scPoli",
      grepl("^Pseudobulk_", method) ~ "Pseudobulk",
      TRUE ~ method # Keep any other simple names as they are
    ),
    
    method_plot = factor(method_plot, 
                         levels = c("ECODA", "Pseudobulk", "MrVI", "GloScope", "GloProp",
                                    "scPoli", "MOFA", "PILOT", "scITD"))
  ) %>%
  
  mutate(method = method %>%
           # Remove _sqrtmat and swap highres/lowres
           stringr::str_replace_all(c("_sqrtmat" = "", 
                                      "highres"  = "HR", 
                                      "lowres"   = "LR")) %>%
           # Transform Pseudobulk_x_PCA_dims -> Pseudobulk_hvg2000_pcadimsx
           stringr::str_replace("Pseudobulk_(\\d+)_PCA_dims", "Pseudobulk_hvg2000_pcadims\\1")
  ) %>%
  
  group_by(method, score) %>%
  mutate(mean_value = mean(value[score == "anosim_score"])) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  
  ggplot(aes(x = fct_reorder(method, mean_value, .desc = TRUE),
             y = value, 
             fill = ifelse(grepl(default_methods_regex, method), "cornflowerblue", "gray70"))) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_se", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar") +
  labs(x = "", y = "") +
  theme_classic2(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_markdown(angle = 90, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
  panel.grid.major.y = element_line(size = 0.25, linetype = 'solid',
                                colour = "lightgrey"), 
  panel.grid.minor.y = element_line(size = 0.25, linetype = 'solid',
                                colour = "lightgrey")) +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  # Highlight default methods
  scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) highlight(
    x,
    default_methods_regex,
    "black")
  ) +
  # ylim(c(-0.4, 1)) +
  facet_grid(scales = "free_x",
             space='free',
             rows = vars(score),
             cols = vars(method_plot), # Use the new grouping column for column faceting
             labeller = labeller(score = score.labs))


ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "Supp_fig_22_methodparameter_screening.pdf"),
       width = 14,
       height = 8)
```

### Combined ECODA and PB

#### Plot distance distribution after different normalization methods applied

```{r}
ecoda <- result_list$bmark$Pelka$ECODA_authors_HR$dist_mat
pb <- result_list$bmark$Pelka$Pseudobulk_hvg2000$dist_mat

hist(ecoda)
hist(pb)

test1 <- ecoda / max(ecoda)
test2 <- pb / max(pb)
hist(test1, main = "ecoda max")
hist(test2, main = "pb max")

test1 <- ecoda / median(ecoda)
test2 <- pb / median(pb)
hist(test1, main = "ecoda median")
hist(test2, main = "pb median")

test1 <- global_quantile_norm_gaussian(ecoda)
test2 <- global_quantile_norm_gaussian(pb)
hist(test1, main = "ecoda quantiles")
hist(test2, main = "pb quantiles")

test1 <- zscore_transform(ecoda)
test2 <- zscore_transform(pb)
hist(test1, main = "ecoda zscore")
hist(test2, main = "pb zscore")
```

#### Plot benchmark including ECODA+PB (overview)

```{r fig.height=9, fig.width=7}
norm_methods <- c("max", "median", "zscore", "quantile")

filter_methods <- c(
  "ECODA_authors_HR",
  "ECODA_authors_HR_NULL",
  
  paste0("ECODA_PB_combo_norm", norm_methods, "_ecodaweight0.5"),
  "ECODA_PB_combo_normcosine_ecodaweight0.5",
  
  "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer2",
  "ECODA_seuratres_2",
  # "ECODA_authors_LR",
  "Avg_PCA_embedding",
  "Freq_highres",
  # "ECODA_deconv",
  "Pseudobulk_10_PCA_dims",
  "MOFA_hvg2000_factors15",
  "scITD_hvg2000_factors5",
  "MrVI_hvg2000",
  "scPoli_hvg2000",
  "PILOT_hvg2000",
  "GloScope_hvg2000_pcadims30_sqrtmat"
)


df_results %>%
  filter(method %in% filter_methods) %>%
  mutate(method = recode(method,
                         "ECODA_authors_HR" = "ECODA_authors",
                         "ECODA_authors_HR_NULL" = "Shuffled_baseline",
                         "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer2" = "ECODA_automated",
                         "ECODA_seuratres_2" = "ECODA_unsupervised",
                         "Pseudobulk_10_PCA_dims" = "Pseudobulk",
                         "Freq_highres" = "Cell_type_composition_%",
                         "MOFA_hvg2000_factors15" = "MOFA",
                         "scITD_hvg2000_factors5" = "scITD",
                         "MrVI_hvg2000" = "MrVI",
                         "scPoli_hvg2000" = "scPoli",
                         "scITD_2_factors" = "scITD",
                         "PILOT_hvg2000" = "PILOT",
                         "GloScope_hvg2000_pcadims30_sqrtmat" = "GloScope")) %>%
  
  group_by(method, score) %>%
  mutate(mean_value = mean(value[score == "anosim_score"])) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  
  ggplot(aes(x = fct_reorder(method, mean_value, .desc = TRUE),
             y = value, 
             fill = ifelse(grepl(default_methods_regex, method), "cornflowerblue", "gray70"))) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_se", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar") +
  labs(x = "", y = "") +
  theme_classic2(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_markdown(angle = 80, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
  panel.grid.major.y = element_line(size = 0.25, linetype = 'solid',
                                colour = "lightgrey"), 
  panel.grid.minor.y = element_line(size = 0.25, linetype = 'solid',
                                colour = "lightgrey")) +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  # ylim(c(-0.4, 1)) +
  facet_grid(scales = "free_x",
             space='free',
             rows = vars(score),
             labeller = labeller(score = score.labs))
```

#### Plot benchmark including ECODA+PB (detailed)

```{r fig.height=7, fig.width=6}
filter_methods <- c(
  "ECODA_authors_HR",
  "ECODA_authors_HR_NULL",
  paste0("ECODA_PB_combo_norm", norm_methods, "_ecodaweight0.5"),
  "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer2",
  "ECODA_seuratres_2",
  # "ECODA_authors_LR",
  "Avg_PCA_embedding",
  "Freq_highres",
  # "ECODA_deconv",
  "Pseudobulk_10_PCA_dims",
  "MOFA_hvg2000_factors15",
  "scITD_hvg2000_factors5",
  "MrVI_hvg2000",
  "scPoli_hvg2000",
  "PILOT_hvg2000",
  "GloScope_hvg2000_pcadims30_sqrtmat"
)


df_plot <- df_results %>%
  filter(method %in% filter_methods) %>%
  mutate(method = recode(method,
                         "ECODA_authors_HR" = "ECODA_authors",
                         "ECODA_authors_HR_NULL" = "Shuffled_baseline",
                         "ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer2" = "ECODA_automated",
                         "ECODA_seuratres_2" = "ECODA_unsupervised",
                         "Pseudobulk_10_PCA_dims" = "Pseudobulk",
                         "Freq_highres" = "Cell_type_composition_%",
                         "MOFA_hvg2000_factors15" = "MOFA",
                         "scITD_hvg2000_factors5" = "scITD",
                         "MrVI_hvg2000" = "MrVI",
                         "scPoli_hvg2000" = "scPoli",
                         "scITD_2_factors" = "scITD",
                         "PILOT_hvg2000" = "PILOT",
                         "GloScope_hvg2000_pcadims30_sqrtmat" = "GloScope"))


df_plot %>%
  filter(score == "anosim_score") %>%
  
  ggplot(aes(x = fct_reorder(method, value, .desc = TRUE, .fun = mean), y = value)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_se", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", fill = "white", color = "black") +
  geom_point(position = "identity", alpha = 0.6, size = 0.8) +
  geom_line(aes(group = dataset), color = "grey20", alpha = 0.2) +
  stat_compare_means(
    ref.group = "ECODA_PB_combo_ecodaweight0.5",
    method = "wilcox.test",
    paired = TRUE,
    angle = 25,
    aes(label = sprintf("%5.2f", as.numeric(..p.format..)))
  ) +
  ylim(c(min(df_plot$value), max(df_plot$value) + 0.05)) +
  labs(x = "", y = "ANOSIM score") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 80, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))



df_plot %>%
  filter(score == "cluster_score") %>%
  
  ggplot(aes(x = fct_reorder(method, value, .desc = TRUE, .fun = mean), y = value)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_se", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", fill = "white", color = "black") +
  geom_point(position = "identity", alpha = 0.6, size = 0.8) +
  geom_line(aes(group = dataset), color = "grey20", alpha = 0.2) +
  stat_compare_means(
    ref.group = "ECODA_PB_combo_ecodaweight0.5",
    method = "wilcox.test",
    paired = TRUE,
    angle = 25,
    aes(label = sprintf("%5.2f", as.numeric(..p.format..)))
  ) +
  ylim(c(min(df_plot$value), max(df_plot$value) + 0.05)) +
  labs(x = "", y = "ARI score") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 65, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))




df_plot %>%
  filter(score == "mod_knn3_score") %>%
  
  ggplot(aes(x = fct_reorder(method, value, .desc = TRUE, .fun = mean), y = value)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_se", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", fill = "white", color = "black") +
  geom_point(position = "identity", alpha = 0.6, size = 0.8) +
  geom_line(aes(group = dataset), color = "grey20", alpha = 0.2) +
  stat_compare_means(
    ref.group = "ECODA_PB_combo_ecodaweight0.5",
    method = "wilcox.test",
    paired = TRUE,
    angle = 25,
    aes(label = sprintf("%5.2f", as.numeric(..p.format..)))
  ) +
  ylim(c(min(df_plot$value), max(df_plot$value) + 0.05)) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Modularity score") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 65, vjust = 1, hjust = 1))
```

### Poster figures

```{r fig.height=10, fig.width=14}
# Poster figure (highlighted)

filter_methods <- c(
  "Pseudobulk",
  # "Freq_highres",
  # "ECODA_deconv",
  # "ECODA_lowres",
  "ECODA_highres",
  # "ECODA_high_res_2_PCA_dims",
  # "ECODA_highres_top_varexp0.7",
  # "ECODA_seuratres_0.4",
  "MOFA_2_factors",
  "scITD_2_factors",
  "MrVI",
  "scPoli",
  "PILOT",
  "GloScope"
)

p <- df_results %>%
  filter(score == "anosim_score") %>%
  filter(method %in% filter_methods) %>%
  mutate(method = recode(method,
                         "MOFA_2_factors" = "MOFA",
                         "scITD_2_factors" = "scITD",
                         "ECODA_highres" = "ECODA")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = fct_reorder(method, value, .desc = TRUE, .fun = mean), y = value, 
             fill = ifelse(method == "ECODA", "lightgreen", "gray70"))) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_se", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar") +
  geom_point(position = "identity", alpha = 0.6, size = 0.7) +
  geom_line(aes(group = dataset), color = "grey20", alpha = 0.1) +
  stat_compare_means(
    ref.group = "ECODA",
    method = "wilcox.test",
    paired = TRUE,
    angle = 25,
    label.y = 0.93,
    aes(label = sprintf("%5.2f", as.numeric(..p.format..)))
  ) +
  ylim(c(-0.1, 1)) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Separation of biological groups\n(ANOSIM score)") +
  theme_classic2(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_markdown(angle = 65, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) highlight(x, "ECODA", "black"))
print(p)
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "POSTER_benchmark_summary_barplot_anosim_mainmethods_refECODA.pdf"),
       width = 3.5,
       height = 4)



filter_methods <- c(
  "Pseudobulk",
  "ECODA_highres",
  "ECODA_highres_top_varexp0.1"
)


p <- df_results %>%
  filter(score == "anosim_score") %>%
  filter(method %in% filter_methods) %>%
  mutate(method = recode(method,
                         "ECODA_highres_top_varexp0" = "2 most highly variable cell types",
                         "ECODA_highres" = "ECODA")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = fct_reorder(method, value, .desc = TRUE, .fun = mean), y = value, 
             fill = ifelse(method == "ECODA", "lightgreen", "gray70"))) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_se", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar") +
  geom_point(position = "identity", alpha = 0.6, size = 0.7) +
  geom_line(aes(group = dataset), color = "grey20", alpha = 0.1) +
  stat_compare_means(
    ref.group = "ECODA",
    method = "wilcox.test",
    paired = TRUE,
    angle = 25,
    label.y = 0.93,
    aes(label = sprintf("%5.2f", as.numeric(..p.format..)))
  ) +
  ylim(c(-0.1, 1)) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Separation of biological groups\n(ANOSIM score)") +
  theme_classic2(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_markdown(angle = 65, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(),
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) highlight(x, "ECODA", "black"))
print(p)
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "benchmark_summary_barplot_anosim_ECODAvs2HVCvsBULK_refECODA.pdf"),
       width = 2,
       height = 6)




filter_methods <- c(
  "ECODA_deconv",
  "ECODA_lowres",
  "ECODA_highres",
  "ECODA_highres_HiTME",
  "ECODA_highres_HiTME_layer3",
  "ECODA_highres_scATOMIC",
  "ECODA_seuratres_0.4"
)

# my_comparisons <- list(
#   c("Low res. annot.", "High res. annot.")
#   )

p <- df_results %>%
  filter(score == "anosim_score") %>%
  filter(method %in% filter_methods) %>%
  mutate(method = recode(method,
                         "ECODA_deconv" = "Deconvoluted",
                         "ECODA_lowres" = "Low res. author annot.",
                         "ECODA_highres" = "High res. author annot.",
                         "ECODA_highres_HiTME" = "HiTME annot.",
                         "ECODA_highres_HiTME_layer3" = "HiTME annot. w. gene. progs.",
                         "ECODA_highres_scATOMIC" = "scATOMIC annot.",
                         "ECODA_seuratres_0.4" = "Unsup. Leiden clust.")) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = fct_reorder(method, value, .desc = TRUE, .fun = mean), y = value, 
             fill = ifelse(method == "High res. author annot.", "lightgreen", "gray70"))) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_se", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar") +
  geom_point(position = "identity", alpha = 0.6, size = 0.5) +
  geom_line(aes(group = dataset), color = "grey20", alpha = 0.1) +
  stat_compare_means(
    # comparisons = my_comparisons,
    ref.group = "High res. author annot.",
    method = "wilcox.test",
    paired = TRUE,
    label.y = 1,
    tip.length = 0,
    aes(label = after_stat(p.signif))
  ) +
  ylim(c(-0.03, 1)) +
  labs(x = "", y = "Separation of biological groups\n(ANOSIM score)") +
  theme_classic2(base_size = 12) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_markdown(angle = 65, vjust = 1, hjust = 1)) +
  scale_fill_identity() +
  scale_x_discrete(labels = function(x) highlight(x, "High res. author annot.", "black"))
print(p)
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "POSTER_benchmark_summary_barplot_anosim_ecodamethods_refECODA_highres.pdf"),
       width = 3,
       height = 5)
```

### Other figures (not shown)

```{r}
ggplot(df_results, aes(x = method, y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = dataset), size = 2) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=1:length(unique((df_results$dataset)))) +
  geom_line(aes(group=dataset, linetype=dataset), alpha = 1, color = "steelblue") +
  labs(x = "", y = "Separation score") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)) +
  facet_grid(scales = "free",
             rows = vars(score),
             labeller = labeller(score = score.labs))
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "benchmark_summary_barplot_separatescores.pdf"),
       width = 7,
       height = 7)



df_results_clustering <- df_results %>%
  filter(score == "cluster_score")

df_results_clustering <- df_results_clustering[!grepl("2|10|PCA", df_results_clustering$method), ]

for (ds in unique(df_results_clustering$dataset)) {
  number_cts <- dim(result_list$bmark[[ds]]$ECODA_highres$feat_mat)[2]
  df_results_clustering[df_results_clustering$dataset == ds, "number_celltypes"] <- number_cts
}

# df_results_clustering$dataset <- paste0(df_results_clustering$dataset, " (", df_results_clustering$number_celltypes, ")")
# legend_title <- "Dataset (number of cell types)"
legend_title <- "Dataset"

# Example assuming 'data' is your dataframe
anova_result <- aov(value ~ method, data = df_results_clustering)
summary(anova_result)
stat.test <- rstatix::tukey_hsd(anova_result)
stat.test
stat.test$p.adj <- round(stat.test$p.adj, 3)


my_comparisons <- purrr::map(unique(as.character(df_results_clustering$method)), ~ c(.x, "Pseudobulk"))
my_comparisons <- Filter(function(x) length(unique(x)) == 2, my_comparisons)
my_comparisons <- my_comparisons[c(3, 6, 4, 5, 2, 1)]


p <- ggplot(df_results_clustering, aes(x = method, y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  stat_compare_means(
    # comparisons = my_comparisons,
    ref.group = "Pseudobulk",
    method = "t.test",
    paired = TRUE,
    angle = 25,
    aes(label = sprintf("p = %5.2f", as.numeric(..p.format..)))) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = dataset), size = 2) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=1:length(unique((df_results_clustering$dataset)))) +
  geom_line(aes(group=dataset, linetype=dataset), alpha = 1, color = "steelblue") +
  labs(x = "", y = "Adjusted Rand Index") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1)) +
  labs(group = legend_title, linetype = legend_title, shape = legend_title) +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 65, vjust = 1, hjust=1))
print(p)
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "benchmark_summary_barplot_clustering.pdf"),
       width = 7,
       height = 5)



df_results_mod <- df_results %>%
  filter(score == "mod_score")

p <- ggplot(df_results_mod, aes(x = method, y = value)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA) +
  stat_compare_means(
    # comparisons = my_comparisons,
    ref.group = "Pseudobulk",
    method = "t.test",
    paired = TRUE,
    angle = 25,
    aes(label = sprintf("p = %5.2f", as.numeric(..p.format..)))
  ) +
  geom_point(aes(shape = dataset), size = 2) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=1:length(unique((df_results_mod$dataset)))) +
  geom_line(aes(group=dataset, linetype=dataset), alpha = 1, color = "steelblue") +
  labs(x = "", y = "Modularity score") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, vjust = 0.5, hjust=1))
print(p)
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "benchmark_summary_barplot_modularity.pdf"),
       width = 7,
       height = 6)
```

## Figure 4 AB: Plot feature selection

Surprisingly, even at the lowest number highly variable cell types (i.e. variance explained of 10% -\> \>=2 cell types) the average biological separation is quite very high. Different daatasets can do with a different number of highly variable cell types (separation plateaus out at different levels of variance explained % and number of cell types retained).

```{r}
df_results_sub <- df_results %>%
  # filter(score == "anosim_score") %>%
  filter(grepl("ECODA_authors_HR", method) & !grepl("HiTME|scATOMIC|PCA|NULL|most|least", method)) %>%
  filter(!grepl("cmv|sex", dataset)) %>%
  filter(!method == "ECODA_authors_HR_top_varexp0") %>%
  mutate(method = recode(method,
                         "ECODA_authors_HR" = "100",
                         "ECODA_authors_HR_top_varexp0" = "2most_varcts")) %>%
  mutate(method = gsub("ECODA_authors_HR", "", method)) %>%
  mutate(method = if_else(
    grepl("^_top_varexp0\\.\\d", method),
    paste0(as.integer(gsub("_top_varexp0\\.", "", method)) * 10),
    method
  )) %>%
  # mutate(method = gsub("top_varexp0", "2most_varcts", method)) %>%
  mutate(method = fct_relevel(method)) %>%
  mutate(method = factor(method, levels = rev(mixedsort(levels(method))))) %>%
  mutate(dataset = recode(dataset,
                          "GongSharma_all_age" = "GongSharma"))

df_results_anosim <- df_results_sub %>% filter(score == "anosim_score")

df_hvc40 <- df_results_anosim[df_results_anosim$method == 40 ,]
perc_tot <- c()
for (ds in df_hvc40$dataset) {
  tot <- df_results_anosim$n_features[df_results_anosim$dataset == ds & df_results_anosim$method == 100]
  perc_tot <- c(perc_tot, round(df_hvc40$n_features[df_hvc40$dataset == ds] / tot * 100))
}

cts <- df_hvc40$n_features
print(paste0("The top ", min(cts), "-", max(cts), " cell types ", 
             "(~", min(perc_tot), "-", max(perc_tot), "% of the total) ",
             "explain 40% of the datasets' variances."))

unique(df_results_sub$method)

pointsize <- 1.5

p1 <- df_results_sub %>%
  filter(score == "anosim_score") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = method, y = n_features)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_se", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", fill = "white", color = "black") +
  geom_point(aes(shape = dataset, color = dataset), size = pointsize) + # Color added for legend consistency
  scale_shape_manual(values=1:length(unique((df_results_sub$dataset)))) +
  geom_line(aes(group=dataset, linetype=dataset, color = dataset), alpha = 1) +
  labs(x = "Variance explained (%)", y = "Number of cell types") +
  theme_classic2() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 65, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "none")
print(p1)

p2 <- df_results_sub %>%
  filter(score == "anosim_score") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = method, y = value)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_se", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", fill = "white", color = "black") +
  geom_point(aes(shape = dataset, color = dataset), size = pointsize) + # Color added for legend consistency
  scale_shape_manual(values=1:length(unique((df_results_sub$dataset)))) +
  geom_line(aes(group=dataset, linetype=dataset, color = dataset), alpha = 1) +
  stat_compare_means(
    ref.group = "All cell types",
    method = "wilcox.test",
    paired = TRUE,
    angle = 25,
    label.y = 0.93,
    aes(label = sprintf("%5.2f", as.numeric(..p.format..)))
  ) +
  # ylim(c(-0.1, 1)) +
  labs(x = "Variance explained (%)", y = "Separation score (ANOSIM)") +
  theme_classic2() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 65, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "none")
print(p2)


# Combine the plots vertically
combined_plot <- (p1 / p2) + 
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'A') & 
  theme(
    plot.tag = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right"
  ) &
  labs(color = "Dataset", 
       shape = "Dataset", 
       linetype = "Dataset")
print(combined_plot)
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "Figure_4_AB_featureselectionHVC_barplot_anosim.pdf"),
       width = 5.5,
       height = 6)
```

#### Supp fig 19: HVC Mod + ARI

```{r fig.height=3.5, fig.width=8}
p3 <- df_results_sub %>%
  filter(score == "mod_knn3_score") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = method, y = value)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_se", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", fill = "white", color = "black") +
  geom_point(aes(shape = dataset, color = dataset), size = pointsize) + # Color added for legend consistency
  scale_shape_manual(values=1:length(unique((df_results_sub$dataset)))) +
  geom_line(aes(group=dataset, linetype=dataset, color = dataset), alpha = 1) +
  stat_compare_means(
    ref.group = "All cell types",
    method = "wilcox.test",
    paired = TRUE,
    angle = 25,
    label.y = 0.93,
    aes(label = sprintf("%5.2f", as.numeric(..p.format..)))
  ) +
  # ylim(c(-0.1, 1)) +
  labs(x = "Variance explained (%)", y = "Separation score (Modularity)") +
  theme_classic2() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 65, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "none")
print(p3)

p4 <- df_results_sub %>%
  filter(score == "cluster_score") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = method, y = value)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = "mean_se", geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "bar", fill = "white", color = "black") +
  geom_point(aes(shape = dataset, color = dataset), size = pointsize) + # Color added for legend consistency
  scale_shape_manual(values=1:length(unique((df_results_sub$dataset)))) +
  geom_line(aes(group=dataset, linetype=dataset, color = dataset), alpha = 1) +
  stat_compare_means(
    ref.group = "All cell types",
    method = "wilcox.test",
    paired = TRUE,
    angle = 25,
    label.y = 0.93,
    aes(label = sprintf("%5.2f", as.numeric(..p.format..)))
  ) +
  # ylim(c(-0.1, 1)) +
  labs(x = "Variance explained (%)", y = "Separation score (ARI)") +
  theme_classic2() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 65, vjust = 1, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "none")
print(p4)


# Combine the plots vertically
combined_plot <- (p3 + p4) + 
  plot_layout(guides = "collect") +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'A') & 
  theme(
    plot.tag = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right"
  ) &
  labs(color = "Dataset", 
       shape = "Dataset", 
       linetype = "Dataset")
print(combined_plot)
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "Supp_fig_19_featureselectionHVC_barplot_mod_ari.pdf"),
       width = 8,
       height = 3.5)
```


### Show example PCAs

#### NOTE: CLR of two parts vs simple log-ratio

The CLR of the first part is exactly half of the log-ratio of the two parts.

```{r}
test <- result_list$bmark[[ds]]$Freq_highres$feat_mat[, colnames(feat_mat_recalc)]
test_clr <- clr(test)
test_lr <- log(test$`Immature B cells` / test$TIM)
```

```{r}
test_clr[, 1]
```

```{r}
test_lr
```

```{r}
test_clr[, 1] * 2 - test_lr
```

#### Figure 4 C: Adams

```{r fig.height=3, fig.width=7}
ds <- "Adams"

feat_mat_recalc <- result_list$bmark[[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR_top_varexp0$feat_mat
feat_mat <- result_list$bmark[[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR$feat_mat[, colnames(feat_mat_recalc)]

labels <- result_list$bmark[[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR$labels
df_logr <- feat_mat_recalc[, 1, drop = FALSE] * 2

df_logr$Groups <- labels
avg_sep_score <- calc_avg_sep_score(dist(feat_mat), labels = labels, digits = 3)

p1 <- plot_pca(
  feat_mat = feat_mat,
  labels = labels,
  cluster_score = FALSE,mod_score = FALSE, anosim_score = FALSE,
  coord_equal = TRUE,
  title = "Adams",
  n_ct_show = 4, invisible = "quali", repel = TRUE, pointsize = 2
)

p2 <- ggplot(df_logr, aes(x = Groups, y = !!sym(colnames(df_logr)[1]), fill = Groups)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +  # Standard boxplot
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 1, alpha = 0.5) + # Optional: adds points to see distribution
  theme_classic() +
  # theme_cleveland() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) + # Rotate labels if group names are long
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = paste0(
      "Log ratio\n",
      colnames(feat_mat_recalc)[1], " / ", colnames(feat_mat_recalc)[2]
    )
  ) &
  NoLegend()


wrap_plots(list(p1, p2)) +
  plot_layout(nrow = 1, widths = c(2.5, 1))
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "Figure_4_C_featureselectionHVC_PCA_adams.pdf"),
       width = 7,
       height = 3)
```

#### Figure 4 D: Kfoury

```{r fig.height=3, fig.width=7}
ds <- "Kfoury"

feat_mat_recalc <- result_list$bmark[[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR_top_varexp0$feat_mat
feat_mat <- result_list$bmark[[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR$feat_mat[, colnames(feat_mat_recalc)]

labels <- result_list$bmark[[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR$labels
df_logr <- feat_mat_recalc[, 1, drop = FALSE] * 2
df_logr$Groups <- labels
avg_sep_score <- calc_avg_sep_score(dist(feat_mat), labels = labels, digits = 3)

p1 <- plot_pca(
  feat_mat = feat_mat,
  labels = labels,
  cluster_score = FALSE,mod_score = FALSE, anosim_score = FALSE,
  coord_equal = TRUE,
  title = "Kfoury",
  n_ct_show = 4, invisible = "quali", repel = TRUE, pointsize = 2
)

p2 <- ggplot(df_logr, aes(x = Groups, y = !!sym(colnames(df_logr)[1]), fill = Groups)) +
  geom_boxplot(outlier.shape = NA, alpha = 0.7) +  # Standard boxplot
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, size = 1, alpha = 0.5) + # Optional: adds points to see distribution
  theme_classic() +
  # theme_cleveland() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) + # Rotate labels if group names are long
  labs(
    x = NULL,
    y = paste0(
      "Log ratio\n",
      colnames(feat_mat_recalc)[1], " / ", colnames(feat_mat_recalc)[2]
    )
  ) &
  NoLegend()

wrap_plots(list(p1, p2)) +
  plot_layout(nrow = 1, widths = c(2.5, 1))
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "Figure_4_D_featureselectionHVC_PCA_kfoury.pdf"),
       width = 7,
       height = 3)
```

## Separation based on least variable cell types (sanity check)

```{r}
# Create an empty list to store the results from each dataset
temp_list <- list()

for (ds in names(result_list[["bmark"]])) {
  feat_mat <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR$feat_mat
  labels <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR$labels
  ncts <- 2
  
  # Calculate separation score for most highly variable cell types (HVC)
  top_hvcs <- get_ct_var(feat_mat, show_plot = FALSE, descending = TRUE) %>%
    get_hvcs(top_n_hvcs = ncts, variance_threshold = NULL)
  score_most_hvc <- calc_sep_score(feat_mat[, top_hvcs], labels)$anosim_score
  
  # Calculate separation score for least highly variable cell types (HVC)
  least_hvcs <- get_ct_var(feat_mat, show_plot = FALSE, descending = FALSE) %>%
    get_hvcs(top_n_hvcs = ncts, variance_threshold = NULL)
  score_least_hvc <- calc_sep_score(feat_mat[, least_hvcs], labels)$anosim_score
  
  # Store the results in a temporary data frame
  temp_df <- data.frame(
    dataset = ds,
    anosim_most_hvc = score_most_hvc,
    anosim_least_hvc = score_least_hvc
  )
  
  # Add the temporary data frame to the results list
  temp_list[[ds]] <- temp_df
}

# Combine all data frames in the list into a single data frame
df_plot <- bind_rows(temp_list)



# Reshape the data from wide to long format for ggplot
df_plot_long <- df_plot %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = starts_with("anosim_"),
    names_to = "group",
    values_to = "anosim_score"
  ) %>%
  # Clean up the group names for better readability
  mutate(group = gsub("anosim_|_hvc", "", group))

# Create the grouped bar plot
ggplot(df_plot_long, aes(x = dataset, y = anosim_score, fill = group)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +
  labs(
    title = "ANOSIM Scores for Highly vs. Least Variable Cell Types",
    x = "Dataset",
    y = "ANOSIM Score",
    fill = "Cell Type Group" # Legend title
  ) +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("most" = "darkred", "least" = "steelblue")) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))

```

## Supp fig 20

## Most highly variable cell types per dataset

```{r fig.height=15, fig.width=15}
# devtools::install_github("carmonalab/scECODA", force = TRUE)
library(scECODA)
plot_list <- list()

dss <- names(result_list[["bmark"]])
# dss <- dss[!grepl("cmv|sex", dss)]
# dss[dss == "GongSharma_all_age"] <- "GongSharma"


for (ds in dss) {
  counts <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR$counts
  meta <- data.frame(sample_id = names(result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR$labels),
                     group = result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR$labels
  )
  ecoda_object <- ecoda(counts, metadata = meta, variance_explained = 0.4)
  
  if (ds == "GongSharma_all_age") {
    ec_plot <- ecoda_object
    pt <- "GongSharma"
  } else pt <- ds
  
  p <- plot_varmean(ecoda_object, plot_title = pt)
  # print(p)
  plot_list <- c(plot_list, p)
  # p <- plot_corr(ecoda_object)
  # print(p)
}

wrap_plots(plot_list) +
  plot_layout(nrow = 4, guides = "collect")

ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "Supp_fig_20_HVCs_per_dataset.pdf"),
       width = 20,
       height = 20)
```


```{r fig.height=5, fig.width=6}
plot_pca_contributions_horizontal(prcomp(ec_plot@clr), pcs = c("PC1", "PC2"), n = 10)
```



```{r fig.height=8, fig.width=12}
print("Most highly variable cell types per dataset:")
for (ds in names(result_list[["bmark"]])) {
  # print(ds)
  # print(paste("ANOSIM score using all cell types:", round(result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR$scores$anosim_score, 3)))
  # print(paste("ANOSIM score at 10% variance explained:", round(result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR_top_varexp0.1$scores$anosim_score, 3)))
  # print("Most highly variable cell types (authors annotation):\n")
  # print(paste(colnames(result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR_top_varexp0$feat_mat)))
  # print("Most highly variable cell types (HiTME annotation):\n")
  # print(paste(colnames(result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]]$ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer3_top_varexp0$feat_mat)))
  
  p <- result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR$feat_mat %>%
    get_ct_var() %>%
    varmeanplot(title = ds, label_points = TRUE)
  print(p)
  
  # p <- plot_pca(
  #   result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR_2least_varcts$feat_mat,
  #   labels = result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR_2least_varcts$labels,
  #   title = paste0(ds, ": Least variable cts"),
  #   invisible = "quali", labelsize = 4)
  # print(p)
  # p <- plot_pca(
  #   result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR_top_varexp0$feat_mat,
  #   labels = result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR_top_varexp0$labels,
  #   title = paste0(ds, ": Most highly variable cts "),
  #   invisible = "quali", labelsize = 4)
  # print(p)
}
```

```{r fig.height=8, fig.width=12}
print("Most highly variable cell types per dataset:")
for (ds in names(result_list[["bmark"]])) {
  print(ds)
  print("Most highly variable cell types (HiTME annotation):\n")
  print(paste(colnames(result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]]$ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer2_top_varexp0.5$feat_mat)))
  print(paste(colnames(result_list[["bmark"]][[ds]]$ECODA_HiTME_HR_layer3_top_varexp0.5$feat_mat)))
}
```

### Check IFN expression in Stephenson dataset

```{r}
ds <- "Stephenson"
seurat <- readRDS(file.path(path_data, paste0(datasets[[ds]][["ds_name"]], seurat_file_ending)))

plot_data <- data.frame(
  expression = seurat$HeatShock_UCell,
  status = seurat$Status
)

ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = expression, fill = status)) +
  geom_density(alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Expression Density by Status",
    x = "IFN_UCell Expression",
    y = "Density",
    fill = "Status"
  ) +
  xlim(c(0, 0.2)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Healthy" = "steelblue", "Covid" = "darkred"))
```

```{r}
metadata <- get_metadata(seurat)
labels <- get_labels(seurat, "Status")
test <- process_coda_fig(seurat, labels, ct_col = "layer2", title = "ECODA\nHiTME layer2")
test

varexp_hvc <- 0.7
test1 <- process_coda_fig(seurat, labels, ECODA_top_varexp_hvct = varexp_hvc, ct_col = "layer2", title = paste0("ECODA layer2\nhigh res. var. exp. ", varexp_hvc*100, "%"))
test1


df_counts <- get_ct_comp_df_seurat(seurat, sample_col = "Sample", "layer2")

feat_mat <- df_counts %>%
  impute_zeros(clr_zero_impute_method = clr_zero_impute_method, clr_zero_impute_num = clr_zero_impute_num) %>%
  calc_perc_df()

feat_mat <- feat_mat %>%
  clr()

get_ct_var(feat_mat, show_plot = FALSE, descending = TRUE) %>%
        get_hvcs(top_n_hvcs = 10, variance_threshold = NULL)
```

### Check different UCell score thresholds for HiTME layer3

```{r}
seurat <- replace_HiTMElayer3_annot(seurat)
```

```{r}
metadata <- get_metadata(seurat)
labels <- get_labels(seurat, "Status")
test2 <- process_coda_fig(seurat, labels, ct_col = "layer3", title = "ECODA\nHiTME layer3")
test2

varexp_hvc <- 0.7
test3 <- process_coda_fig(seurat, labels, ECODA_top_varexp_hvct = varexp_hvc, ct_col = "layer3", title = paste0("ECODA layer3\nhigh res. var. exp. ", varexp_hvc*100, "%"))
test3


df_counts <- get_ct_comp_df_seurat(seurat, sample_col = "Sample", "layer3")

feat_mat <- df_counts %>%
  impute_zeros(clr_zero_impute_method = clr_zero_impute_method, clr_zero_impute_num = clr_zero_impute_num) %>%
  calc_perc_df()

feat_mat <- feat_mat %>%
  clr()

get_ct_var(feat_mat, show_plot = FALSE, descending = TRUE) %>%
        get_hvcs(top_n_hvcs = 10, variance_threshold = NULL)
```

## Reduced separation with fewer cell types?

Why do some datasets show reduced separability when using fewer highly variable cell types while other still show good separation even with just 2 cell types? Are there any comonalities between these datasets?

In general, datasets with HIGH ANOSIM score (\~ \>0.5) when using all cell types also show still good separability with very low number of cell types. -\> Maybe these biological conditions are more distinct and are easier to separate? Datasets with LOW ANOSIM score (\~ \<0.5) when using all cell types also show decreasing separability when reducing the number of cell types. -\> The minimum number of cell types for maximal separability depends on the dataset

```{r}
df_results_sub <- df_results %>%
  filter(score == "anosim_score") %>%
  filter(grepl("ECODA_hi", method) & !grepl("HiTME|scATOMIC|PCA", method)) %>%
  mutate(method = gsub("ECODA_highres_", "", method)) %>%
  mutate(method = if_else(
    grepl("^top_varexp0\\.\\d", method),
    paste0(as.integer(gsub("top_varexp0\\.", "", method)) * 10),
    method
  )) %>%
  mutate(method = gsub("ECODA_highres", "100", method)) %>%
  mutate(method = fct_relevel(method)) %>%
  mutate(method = factor(method, levels = mixedsort(levels(method))))

ds_diffs <- df_results_sub %>%
  filter(method %in% c("10", "70")) %>%
  group_by(dataset) %>%
  summarize(
    diff = value[method == "10"] - value[method == "70"]
  ) %>%
  arrange(diff)

ggplot(ds_diffs, aes(x = reorder(dataset, diff), y = diff)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  labs(x = "Dataset", y = "Difference") +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)) +
  ggtitle("Which datasets have lower separation when using less cell types?\nVar. exp. 10% - Var. exp. 70%")
```

## Which datasets HiTME annotation underperforms?

