```{r}
         title = "Pseudobulk - Batch groups"
)
print(plot_list[["p_pb_batch"]])
```

# Figure 5: Combine all plots

```{r fig.height=6.5, fig.width=10}
wrap_plots(plot_list) +
  plot_layout(nrow = 2) +
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'A') #&
  # theme(
  #   # legend.position='bottom',
  #   plot.tag = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold")
  # )
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "Figure_5_batcheffect_Joanito_PCA.pdf"),
       width = 10,
       height = 6.5)
```

## Number of cells, total, per sample

```{r}
print(paste("Number of samples:", length(unique(seurat@meta.data$sample.ID))))
print(paste("Number of cells:", nrow(seurat@meta.data)))
print(paste("Number of cells per sample:", round(mean(table((seurat@meta.data$sample.ID)))), "+-", round(sd(table((seurat@meta.data$sample.ID))))))
print(paste("Number of cell types:", length(unique(seurat@meta.data$cell.type))))
```

# ---------------------------------------------------------------

# Stephenson dataset

## Load/preprocess

```{r}
p <- file.path(path_nas, "StephensonE_2021_39313646", "output", "StephensonE_2021_33879890_preprocessed.rds")

seurat <- readRDS(p)

seurat <- subset(seurat, subset = Status %in% c("Healthy", "Covid"))

seurat$Days_from_onset <- as.character(seurat$Days_from_onset)
seurat$Days_from_onset[seurat$Days_from_onset == "Healthy"] <- 0
seurat$Days_from_onset[seurat$Days_from_onset == "nan"] <- 999
seurat$Days_from_onset[seurat$Days_from_onset == "Not_known"] <- 999
seurat$Days_from_onset <- as.numeric(seurat$Days_from_onset)
seurat <- subset(seurat, subset = Days_from_onset < 10)


meta <- seurat@meta.data %>%
  group_by(Sample) %>%
  slice(1) %>%
  as.data.frame()
row.names(meta) <- meta$Sample

pb <- get_pb(seurat, sample_col = "Sample")
metadata <- get_metadata(seurat, sample_col = "Sample")
pb_norm <- t(DESeq2.normalize(pb, metadata = metadata, n_hvg = 2000))

bio_col <- "Status"
batch_col <- "Site"


ct_remove <- c(
  "Platelets",
  "RBC",
  "C1_CD16_mono",
  "CD14_mono",
  "CD16_mono"
)
seurat_sub <- subset(seurat, subset = full_clustering %notin% ct_remove)
pb <- get_pb(seurat_sub, sample_col = "Sample")
metadata <- get_metadata(seurat, sample_col = "Sample")
pb_norm_sub <- t(DESeq2.normalize(pb, metadata = metadata, n_hvg = 2000))

plot_list <- list()
```


## Plot PCA

### ECODA

```{r}
dist_mat <- as.data.frame.matrix(table(seurat@meta.data$Sample, seurat@meta.data$full_clustering)) %>%
  # select(-contains("Epithelial")) %>%
  impute_zeros(clr_zero_impute_method = "counts_all", clr_zero_impute_num = 0.5) %>%
  clr() %>%
  dist()

plot_mds(dist_mat, labels = meta[[bio_col]],
         cluster_score = F, mod_score = F, anosim_score = T
)


plot_mds(dist_mat, labels = meta[[batch_col]],
         cluster_score = F, mod_score = F, anosim_score = T
)
```


#### Subset to remove batch effect cell types

```{r}
dist_mat <- as.data.frame.matrix(table(seurat_sub@meta.data$Sample, seurat_sub@meta.data$full_clustering)) %>%
  # select(-contains("Epithelial")) %>%
  impute_zeros(clr_zero_impute_method = "counts_all", clr_zero_impute_num = 0.5) %>%
  clr() %>%
  dist()

plot_list[["p_ecoda_bio_sub"]] <- plot_mds(dist_mat, labels = meta[[bio_col]],
         cluster_score = F, mod_score = F, anosim_score = T
)
print(plot_list[["p_ecoda_bio_sub"]])
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "batcheffect_Stephenson_PCA_ECODA_subset_bio.pdf"),
       width = 4,
       height = 3)


plot_list[["p_ecoda_batch_sub"]] <- plot_mds(dist_mat, labels = meta[[batch_col]],
         cluster_score = F, mod_score = F, anosim_score = T
)
print(plot_list[["p_ecoda_batch_sub"]])
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "batcheffect_Stephenson_PCA_ECODA_subset_batch.pdf"),
       width = 4,
       height = 3)
```




### PB

```{r}
plot_mds(dist(pb_norm), labels = meta[[bio_col]],
         cluster_score = F, mod_score = F, anosim_score = T
)

plot_mds(dist(pb_norm), labels = meta[[batch_col]],
         cluster_score = F, mod_score = F, anosim_score = T
)
```

#### Subset to remove batch effect cell types

```{r}
plot_list[["p_pb_bio_sub"]] <- plot_mds(dist(pb_norm_sub), labels = meta[[bio_col]],
         cluster_score = F, mod_score = F, anosim_score = T
)
print(plot_list[["p_pb_bio_sub"]])
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "batcheffect_Stephenson_PCA_PB_subset_bio.pdf"),
       width = 4,
       height = 3)

plot_list[["p_pb_batch_sub"]] <- plot_mds(dist(pb_norm_sub), labels = meta[[batch_col]],
         cluster_score = F, mod_score = F, anosim_score = T
)
print(plot_list[["p_pb_batch_sub"]])
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "batcheffect_Stephenson_PCA_PB_subset_batch.pdf"),
       width = 4,
       height = 3)
```


#### With blacklist

```{r}
pb_norm_sub <- get_pb_deseq2(seurat_sub, sample_col = "Sample", black_list = "default")

plot_mds(dist(pb_norm_sub), labels = meta[[bio_col]],
         cluster_score = F, mod_score = F, anosim_score = T
)
# ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "batcheffect_Joanito_PCA_PB_bio.pdf"),
#        width = 4,
#        height = 3)

plot_mds(dist(pb_norm_sub), labels = meta[[batch_col]],
         cluster_score = F, mod_score = F, anosim_score = T
)
# ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "batcheffect_Joanito_PCA_PB_batch.pdf"),
#        width = 4,
#        height = 3)

```

# Supp fig 22: Combine all plots

```{r fig.height=15, fig.width=10}
wrap_plots(plot_list) +
  plot_layout(nrow = 2)+
  plot_annotation(tag_levels = 'A') &
  theme(
    # legend.position='bottom',
    plot.tag = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold")
  )
ggsave(file.path(path_plots, "Supp_fig_22_batcheffect_Stephenson_PCA_combined.pdf"),
       width = 10,
       height = 8)
```




# ---------------------------------------------------------------
# ---------------------------------------------------------------

# Export tables with raw data for paper

```{r}
library(writexl)
write_xlsx(df_results_list, path = file.path(path_data, "raw_data_for_publication.xlsx"))
```


# ---------------------------------------------------------------
# ---------------------------------------------------------------

# Info about datasets

```{r}
for (ds in names(result_list$bmark)) {
  print("-----------------------------------------")
  print(ds)
  feat_mat <- result_list$bmark[[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR$feat_mat
  print(paste("Number of cell types:", ncol(feat_mat)))
  print(paste("Number of samples:", nrow(feat_mat)))
  print(paste("Number of cells:", exec_times[exec_times$dataset == ds, "n_cells"][1]))
  
  print(paste("ANOSIM using all cell types:", round(result_list$bmark[[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR$scores$anosim_score, 3)))
  print(paste("ANOSIM at 10% HVC:", round(result_list$bmark[[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR_top_varexp0.1$scores$anosim_score, 3)))
  print(paste("Number of HVC at 10% HVC:", ncol(result_list$bmark[[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR_top_varexp0.1$feat_mat)))
  cat("Most highly variable cell types:", colnames(result_list$bmark[[ds]]$ECODA_authors_HR_top_varexp0$feat_mat), "\n")
  
  cat("Cell types:", colnames(feat_mat), "\n")
}
```

# Supp table 1:
## Number of cells, total, per sample

```{r}
for (ds in names(datasets)) {
  print("-----------------------------------------")
  print(ds)
  seurat <- readRDS(file.path(path_data, paste0(datasets[[ds]][["ds_name"]], seurat_file_ending)))
  print(paste("Number of samples:", length(unique(seurat@meta.data$Sample))))
  print(paste("Number of cells:", nrow(seurat@meta.data)))
  print(paste("Number of cells per sample:", round(mean(table((seurat@meta.data$Sample)))), "+-", round(sd(table((seurat@meta.data$Sample))))))
  print(paste("Number of cell types:", length(unique(seurat@meta.data[[datasets[[ds]][["hi_res_ct_col"]]]]))))
}
```


# ---------------------------------------------------------------
# ---------------------------------------------------------------

# Test Mass Cytometry data

```{r}
df <- readxl::read_xlsx("/Users/christianhalter/Desktop/ECODA_paper/mmc5.xlsx", skip = 2)

# 2. Define Column Groups
broad_ct_cols  <- colnames(df)[5:9]
immune_ct_cols <- colnames(df)[14:21]
cd4_ct_cols    <- c("T01", "T02", "T03", "T04", "T08", "T09", "T13", "T17", "T18", "T20")
cd8_ct_cols    <- c("T05", "T06", "T07", "T10", "T11", "T12", "T14", "T15", "T16", "T19")
epi_ct_cols    <- paste0("Ep", sprintf("%02d", 1:45))
mye_ct_cols    <- paste0("M", sprintf("%02d", 1:19))

# 3. Calculate Compositions (% of Total Sample)
df_compositions <- df %>%
  mutate(
    # --- Immune Level ---
    # Convert Immune Subsets (T-cells, B-cells, etc.) from % of Immune to % of Total
    across(all_of(immune_ct_cols), 
           ~ .x * (`ImmuneCells [%]` / 100), 
           .names = "{.col}_TotalPct"),
    
    # --- T-Cell Lineage Level ---
    # Calculate CD4 and CD8 as % of Total Sample
    # Formula: (Lineage % of T) * (T % of Immune) * (Immune % of Total)
    TCells_CD4_TotalPct = (`TCells CD4+ [%]` / 100) * (`TCells [%]` / 100) * `ImmuneCells [%]`,
    TCells_CD8_TotalPct = (`TCells CD8+ [%]` / 100) * (`TCells [%]` / 100) * `ImmuneCells [%]`,
    
    # --- Cluster Level ---
    # T-Cell Clusters (relative to their respective CD4/CD8 parent)
    across(all_of(cd4_ct_cols), 
           ~ .x * TCells_CD4_TotalPct, 
           .names = "{.col}_TotalPct"),
    
    across(all_of(cd8_ct_cols), 
           ~ .x * TCells_CD8_TotalPct, 
           .names = "{.col}_TotalPct"),
    
    # Myeloid Clusters (relative to the Myeloid parent)
    # Formula: (Cluster Fraction) * (Myeloid % of Immune) * (Immune % of Total)
    across(all_of(mye_ct_cols), 
           ~ .x * (`MyeloidCells [%]` / 100) * `ImmuneCells [%]`, 
           .names = "{.col}_TotalPct"),
    
    # Epithelial Clusters (relative to the Epithelial parent)
    # Formula: (Cluster Fraction) * (Epithelial % of Total)
    across(all_of(epi_ct_cols), 
           ~ .x * `EpithelialCells [%]`, 
           .names = "{.col}_TotalPct")
  )

# 4. Verification Check
# The sum of all Epithelial cluster TotalPcts should equal the original EpithelialCells [%]
df_compositions %>% 
  select(Sample_ID = 1, `EpithelialCells [%]`, starts_with("Ep")) %>%
  mutate(Sum_Ep_Clusters = rowSums(select(., ends_with("_TotalPct")))) %>%
  head()


# 2. Load the clinical metadata
# Adjust the path to where your mmc2.xlsx is located
clinical_df <- readxl::read_xlsx("/Users/christianhalter/Desktop/ECODA_paper/mmc2.xlsx", skip = 2)

# 3. Join the dataframes
# We match 'Patient' from df (mmc5) with 'Patient ID' from clinical_df (mmc2)
df_combined <- df %>%
  left_join(clinical_df, by = c("Patient" = "Patient ID"))

# 4. (Optional) Move metadata columns
# If you want to see the Clinical Subtype or Grade immediately after the Patient ID:
df <- df_combined %>%
  relocate(any_of(c("Clinical Subtype", "Histopathology", "Grade")), .after = Patient)
```

```{r}
# broad_ct_cols  <- colnames(df)[5:9]
# immune_ct_cols <- colnames(df)[14:21]
# cd4_ct_cols    <- c("T01", "T02", "T03", "T04", "T08", "T09", "T13", "T17", "T18", "T20")
# cd8_ct_cols    <- c("T05", "T06", "T07", "T10", "T11", "T12", "T14", "T15", "T16", "T19")
# epi_ct_cols    <- paste0("Ep", sprintf("%02d", 1:45))
# mye_ct_cols    <- paste0("M", sprintf("%02d", 1:19))

feat_mat <- df[, mye_ct_cols]*100

min_val <- min(feat_mat[feat_mat > 0], na.rm = TRUE)
impute_val <- (2/3) * min_val
feat_mat[feat_mat == 0] <- impute_val

feat_mat <- clr(feat_mat)

plot_pca(feat_mat, labels = df$`Clinical Subtype`,
         cluster_score = FALSE, mod_score = FALSE, anosim_score = FALSE,invisible = "quali", n_ct_show = 5)
```

```{r}
# 1. Define Column Groups
broad_ct_cols  <- colnames(df)[5:9] 
immune_sub_cols <- c("NaturalKillerCells [%]", "Granulocytes [%]", "BCells [%]", 
                     "PlasmaCells [%]", "plasmacytoidDendriticCells [%]", "Basophils [%]")

cd4_ct_cols    <- c("T01", "T02", "T03", "T04", "T08", "T09", "T13", "T17", "T18", "T20")
cd8_ct_cols    <- c("T05", "T06", "T07", "T10", "T11", "T12", "T14", "T15", "T16", "T19")
mye_ct_cols    <- paste0("M", sprintf("%02d", 1:19))
epi_ct_cols    <- paste0("Ep", sprintf("%02d", 1:45))

# 2. Calculate Absolute Abundances (% of Total Sample)
df_abs <- df %>%
  mutate(
    # Base components
    Endo_Abs = `EndothelialCells [%]`,
    
    # Immune subsets as % of total sample
    across(all_of(immune_sub_cols), 
           ~ .x * (`ImmuneCells [%]` / 100), 
           .names = "{.col}_Abs"),
    
    # T-cell Lineages as % of total sample
    T_CD4_Abs = (`TCells CD4+ [%]` / 100) * (`TCells [%]` / 100) * `ImmuneCells [%]`,
    T_CD8_Abs = (`TCells CD8+ [%]` / 100) * (`TCells [%]` / 100) * `ImmuneCells [%]`,
    
    # Granular Clusters as % of total sample
    across(all_of(cd4_ct_cols), ~ .x * T_CD4_Abs, .names = "{.col}_Abs"),
    across(all_of(cd8_ct_cols), ~ .x * T_CD8_Abs, .names = "{.col}_Abs"),
    across(all_of(mye_ct_cols), ~ .x * (`MyeloidCells [%]` / 100) * `ImmuneCells [%]`, .names = "{.col}_Abs"),
    across(all_of(epi_ct_cols), ~ .x * `EpithelialCells [%]`, .names = "{.col}_Abs")
  )

# 3. Define the final mutually exclusive feature set 
# Note: "Other_Abs" and Fibroblasts are NOT included here
feat_cols <- c(
  "Endo_Abs",
  paste0(immune_sub_cols, "_Abs"),
  paste0(c(cd4_ct_cols, cd8_ct_cols, mye_ct_cols, epi_ct_cols), "_Abs")
)

# 4. Create the renormalized feat_mat (Sums to 100%)
feat_mat <- df_abs %>%
  # Sum only the selected granular components (excludes Fibro and Other)
  mutate(Normalization_Sum = rowSums(select(., all_of(feat_cols)))) %>%
  # Renormalize to 100
  mutate(across(all_of(feat_cols), ~ (.x / Normalization_Sum) * 100)) %>%
  select(`Sample ID`, all_of(feat_cols))

# Clean up column names
colnames(feat_mat) <- gsub("_Abs| \\[%\\]", "", colnames(feat_mat))

# 5. Impute Zeros with 2/3 of the minimum non-zero value
# We do this on the numeric columns only
numeric_cols <- colnames(feat_mat)[-1]
feat_values <- as.matrix(feat_mat[, numeric_cols])

min_nonzero <- min(feat_values[feat_values > 0], na.rm = TRUE)
feat_values[feat_values == 0] <- (2/3) * min_nonzero

# Final Matrix Construction
feat_mat[, numeric_cols] <- as.data.frame(feat_values)
# Remove Sample ID if you only want the numerical matrix
# feat_mat <- feat_mat[,-1] 

# Final Check
print(range(rowSums(feat_mat[, -1]))) # Should be exactly 100 to 100

feat_mat <- feat_mat[,-1]
```

```{r}
feat_mat <- clr(feat_mat)

plot_pca(feat_mat, labels = df$Tissue, cluster_score = FALSE, mod_score = FALSE, anosim_score = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# BiocManager::install("HDCytoData")
library(HDCytoData)
d_SE <- Krieg_Anti_PD_1_SE() # Returns SummarizedExperiment

d_SE@assays@data$exprs

# 1. Generate a contingency table directly from colData
# This counts cells per population_id for each sample_id
counts_table <- table(d_SE$sample_id, d_SE$population_id)

# 2. Convert to proportions (row-wise normalization)
# prop.table(x, 1) divides each cell by the row sum
feat_mat_pct <- prop.table(counts_table, margin = 1) * 100

# 3. Handle Imputation
min_nonzero <- min(feat_mat_pct[feat_mat_pct > 0])
feat_mat_pct[feat_mat_pct == 0] <- (2/3) * min_nonzero

# 4. Attach Sample-level Metadata
# Instead of distinct(), we can grab the unique mapping from the SE metadata
sample_info <- colData(d_SE) %>%
  as.data.frame() %>%
  select(sample_id, condition, patient_id) %>%
  unique()

# Final merge
feat_mat_final <- as.data.frame(feat_mat_pct) %>%
  rename(sample_id = Var1, cell_type = Var2, percentage = Freq) %>%
  pivot_wider(names_from = cell_type, values_from = percentage) %>%
  left_join(sample_info, by = "sample_id")

feat_mat <- clr(feat_mat)

plot_pca(feat_mat, labels = df$`Clinical Subtype`,
         cluster_score = FALSE, mod_score = FALSE, anosim_score = FALSE,invisible = "quali", n_ct_show = 5)
```
